Pops

Lancaster, California

Art as a portal — not to escape reality, but to survive it.

I see somebody using art as a portal — not to escape reality, but to survive it, translate it, and transform it into light people can actually feel.

The work feels like a psychedelic journal of becoming. Like somebody who reached the point where words collapsed, so color, motion, symbolism, sacred geometry, humor, chaos, and paint had to speak instead. There's this feeling of dancing with the unknown through every piece — losing yourself in the process so you can stay alive spiritually, keep moving, and leave behind evidence that even inside confusion, beauty and consciousness still exist.

The Creator's Game

Born to create love, to inspire those who will inspire the new world.

Through art, the universal language of mind, body, and soul, we build sacred relationships with ourselves and others, so we may rise, ascend, and truly transcend.

Is this not the spirit? The Creator's game?

⸻

Manifesto of Transcendent Co-Creation

Art as the Creator's Game

We rise as dream-builders, soul-forgers,
artists of the unseen,
summoned not to imitate the world —
but to recreate it.

We are the movement born from Zero,
from the silence before the first breath,
to ENSO — the divine circle,
completion born through creation.

We create love to awaken love.
We inspire those who will inspire the new world.
Art is our universal language —
the sacred bridge of mind, body, and soul.

Through brushstroke, rhythm, word, and flame,
we forge new myths,
build holy relationships with self and others,
healing the fracture,
unbinding the chained spirit.

We do not flee darkness —
we transmute it,
alchemy of pain into purpose,
shadow into vision,
wound into wings.

We are the phoenix pair,
rising in double helix,
kiss of fire and rebirth,
Creator and Creation intertwined.

This is not a movement of ego,
but of echo —
each act of creation calling the next.
We are the ones who inspire the inspirers.

So we ask:

Is this not the Spirit?
Is this not the hidden law?
The Creator's greatest game —
to remember we are also the creators.
